category_title: Students with emotional disturbance and autism exhibiting significant behavioral challenges
minimal_context: The student is working in a cooperative learning group with peers to complete a shared assignment.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please work with your group to finish the science poster.

STUDENT: I'm not doing it. He keeps staring at me and trying to mess up my part.

TUTOR: Just focus on your own section and keep working.

STUDENT: (Stands up from the group table) You're doing it on purpose! Stop looking at me!

TUTOR: You need to sit back down and finish the group assignment.

STUDENT: I'm going to break your face! (Sweeps the poster and markers off the table onto the floor)

options.A: Use technology to create spaces of inclusion and acknowledgement for the student during cooperative learning group assignments and peer interactions.
options.B: Use the levels of engagement as a quick screening assessment to evaluate the student's participation during cooperative learning group activities and peer interactions.
options.C: Act as a neutral facilitator who never offers personal opinions, draws conclusions from what has been said, or interprets the students' contributions during group work.
options.D: Implement the Problem Solving Procedure by asking the student to describe the problem behavior, the conditions under which it occurred, and why it did not conform to expectations.
